body:
It looks like our Powershell adapter does not directly check the exit status. Instead, it does a series of checks to see if it should set the exit status.

I'm not really a pwsh programmer, but some poking around suggests that the issue stems from [Line 135 of the adapter script](https://github.com/starship/starship/blob/master/src/init/starship.ps1#L135). Specifically, the check that needs to succeed here to trigger an error (and is currently failing is)

```
$lastCmd.CommandLine -eq $lastCmdletError.Line
```

With your example, `$lastCmd.CommandLine` is `Test` and `$lastCmdletError.Line` is `throw "Error"`, so it seems that throwing an exception is causing the logic of this check to fail.

As to how to fix this, I'm not confident enough with powershell to suggest a fix that doesn't break other things, but hopefully someone else has an idea.
